hint: e- added: Spanish can't start s+consonant
drop t from este → ese = that
used for photos: tomar una foto

front: Can you give me a hand?
Could you give me a hand?
They should give me a hand.
They should give us a hand.
I would like to take a photo.
But I don't have my camera.
I'm taking a photo of the planet.
back: Estoy tomando una foto del planeta.

front: I'd like to take a photo tonight.
back: Me gustaría tomar una foto esta noche.

front: Could you give us a hand tonight?
back: ¿Nos podrías dar una mano esta noche?

front: I don't want that car, it's too big.
back: No quiero ese auto, es muy grande.

front: to interest / to be interesting to
It would interest me to see it.
Does it interest you to dance tonight?
It doesn't seem right to me.
Doesn't it seem right to you?
It seems he doesn't like it.
back: Me parece que no le gusta.

front: It seems he doesn't want to go out tonight.
back: Parece que no quiere salir esta noche.

front: It interests them to go out.
Would it interest you to go out?
I want to put it here.
I want to put salt on it.
I'd like to put a little more salt.
back: Me gustaría ponerle un poco más sal.

front: I'd like to put a little more salt on them.
back: Me gustaría ponerles un poco más sal.

front: Does it interest her to go out to dance tonight?
back: ¿Le interesa salir a bailar esta noche?

front: It seems good to me. (Me parece bueno)
I'd like to put more salt here.
back: Me gustaría ponerle más sal aquí.

front: It doesn't interest them at all.
Why doesn't it seem right to him?
I have been able to eat.
I haven't been able to eat.
I would have been able to go out.
I wouldn't have been able to go out.
They were trying to find it for me. (encontrármelo)
hint: me + lo stacked: me first, lo second

front: He used to dance every day.
We were waiting for you guys.
They were trying to find it for me. (para mí)
I haven't been able to find it.
